title: SAVOURY BUCKWHEAT PORRIDGE
description: Savoury buckwheat porridge is a warm, nourishing dish made with soft-cooked buckwheat, sautéed onions, garlic, and a touch of olive oil. Lightly seasoned and easily customizable with vegetables or protein, it’s a comforting and versatile option for a healthy breakfast or light meal.
benefits: Rich in nutrients and naturally gluten-free
Supports digestion and gut health
Helps maintain steady energy levels
High in plant-based protein and fiber
Heart-friendly and supports cholesterol control
Made with simple, clean, and wholesome ingredients
Easily customizable with vegetables and proteins
steps: Rinse the buckwheat thoroughly under running water and drain well.
Heat olive oil in a pan, add chopped onions and garlic, and sauté until soft and fragrant.
Add buckwheat to the pan and lightly roast for 2–3 minutes for a nuttier flavor.
Pour in water or vegetable broth, add salt and pepper, and let it simmer on low heat until soft and creamy
Add optional vegetables like mushrooms or spinach and cook for a few more minutes. Serve   warm with herbs or a fried egg on top.

title: BUCKWHEAT CHILLA
description: Buckwheat chilla is a savory, protein-rich pancake made with buckwheat flour, finely chopped onions, fresh herbs, and mild spices. Light, wholesome, and quick to prepare, it’s a delicious option for breakfast or a healthy snack.
benefits: Rich in fiber for improved digestion
Packed with healthy fats from nuts and seeds
Provides sustained energy throughout the day
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
High in plant-based protein
Supports heart health
Crunchy, satisfying, and nutrient-dense
Perfect as a quick, healthy snack or breakfast option
steps: In a bowl, mix buckwheat flour, chopped onion, tomato, green chili, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, and salt.
Add water gradually and make a smooth batter with a medium-thick consistency.
Heat a non-stick pan and lightly grease it with oil.
Pour a ladle of batter onto the pan and spread it gently like a pancake.
Cook on both sides until golden brown and crisp. Serve hot with chutney or yogurt.

title: BUCKWHEAT PANCAKES
description: Buckwheat pancakes are soft, wholesome pancakes made from a smooth buckwheat flour batter cooked on a flat pan. Naturally gluten-free and mildly nutty in flavor, they are a healthy and satisfying option for breakfast or a light meal.
benefits: Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
Rich in fiber for better digestion and fullness
Provides steady, long-lasting energy
Supports heart health and cholesterol balance
Contains essential nutrients like magnesium and iron
Light yet satisfying—perfect for breakfast
Can be made sweet or savory for versatility
Made with simple, wholesome ingredients
steps: In a bowl, combine buckwheat flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add milk, egg, mashed banana, honey, and vanilla extract. Mix well until smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan and lightly grease it with butter.
Pour small portions of batter onto the pan and cook until bubbles form on top.
Flip and cook the other side until golden brown. Serve warm with fruits or extra honey.

title: BUCKWHEAT GRANOLA MIX
description: Buckwheat granola mix is a crunchy, wholesome blend of roasted buckwheat combined with nuts and seeds. Lightly toasted for a nutty flavor, it’s a nutritious and versatile option perfect for breakfast, snacking, or topping yogurt and smoothies.
benefits: Rich in fiber for improved digestion
Packed with healthy fats from nuts and seeds
Provides sustained energy throughout the day
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
High in plant-based protein
Supports heart health
Crunchy, satisfying, and nutrient-dense
Perfect as a quick, healthy snack or breakfast option
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, mix buckwheat groats, rolled oats, almonds, pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.
Add honey and melted coconut oil, then mix well until everything is evenly coated.
Spread the mixture evenly on the baking tray and bake for 20–25 minutes, stirring halfway through.
Let it cool completely, then mix in raisins and store in an airtight container.

title: BUCKWHEAT PILAF
description: A wholesome and nutritious lunch dish made with roasted buckwheat, fresh vegetables, and mild seasonings. With its light nutty flavor and satisfying texture, it is a healthy, fiber-rich meal that keeps you full and energized throughout the day.
benefits: Rich in fiber and supports healthy digestion
High in plant-based protein for longer fullness
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
Helps maintain steady blood sugar levels
Supports heart health with magnesium and antioxidants
Packed with iron, zinc, and essential B vitamins
Helps in healthy weight management
Boosts energy levels throughout the day
steps: Rinse the buckwheat thoroughly and drain well.
Heat olive oil in a pan, add chopped onion and garlic, and sauté until soft.
Add carrot and capsicum, then cook for a few minutes until slightly tender.
Add buckwheat groats and lightly roast for 2–3 minutes. Pour in vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper.
Cover and cook on low heat until the buckwheat becomes soft and fluffy. Garnish with fresh herbs and serve warm.

title: BUCKWHEAT CHICKEN ONE-POT
description: A wholesome and protein rich meal made with buckwheat, tender chicken, fresh vegetables, and mild seasonings cooked together in one pot. This nutritious dish offers a perfect balance of flavor, energy, and comfort, making it an ideal healthy lunch or dinner option.
benefits: High in protein for muscle strength and fullness
Rich in fiber and supports healthy digestion
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
Helps maintain steady energy levels
Supports heart health and metabolism
Packed with iron, magnesium, and B vitamins
Balanced meal with protein, carbs, and nutrients
steps: Cut the chicken into small pieces and season with salt and black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a pot, add garlic and onion, and sauté until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces and cook until lightly browned. Then add carrot and capsicum.
Add buckwheat groats and pour in vegetable broth. Mix well and cover.
Cook on low heat until the buckwheat is soft and the chicken is fully cooked. Garnish with fresh herbs and serve warm.

title: BUCKWHEAT VEGETABLE PULAO
description: A healthy and flavorful meal made with buckwheat, fresh vegetables, aromatic herbs, and mild spices. This wholesome dish is light, filling, and rich in fiber, making it a perfect choice for a balanced lunch.
benefits: Rich in fiber and supports healthy digestion
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
Helps maintain steady energy levels
Packed with vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants
Supports heart health and better metabolism
Helps in weight management and longer fullness
Good source of plant-based protein
Light, nutritious, and perfect for everyday meals
steps: Rinse the buckwheat thoroughly and keep aside
Heat oil, sauté cumin, onion, garlic.
Add chopped carrot, beans, peas, and tomato. Cook for a few minutes until slightly soft
Add buckwheat groats, salt, and black pepper. Pour in water and mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat until the buckwheat is soft and fluffy. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve warm.

title: BUCKWHEAT LENTIL BOWL
description: A wholesome and protein-rich meal made with nutritious buckwheat, cooked lentils, fresh vegetables, and light seasonings. This balanced bowl is rich in fiber, plant-based protein, and essential nutrients, making it a healthy and satisfying option for lunch or dinner.
benefits: High in plant-based protein for longer fullness
Rich in fiber and supports healthy digestion
Naturally gluten-free and easy to digest
Helps maintain steady energy levels
Supports heart health and better metabolism
Packed with iron, magnesium, and B vitamins
Helps in weight management and reduced cravings
Nutritious, filling, and perfect for balanced meals
steps: Cook buckwheat and lentils separately.
Sauté vegetables in oil.
Mix everything together.
Season well and add lemon juice.
Serve warm or as bowl meal.

title: BUCKWHEAT DATE ENERGY BALLS
description: A healthy and naturally sweet snack made with buckwheat, dates, nuts, and seeds. These no-bake energy balls are rich in fiber, healthy fats, and natural sugars, making them a perfect quick bite for instant energy and guilt-free snacking.

Rich in fiber and supports healthy digestion
Naturally sweet with no refined sugar
Packed with healthy fats and plant-based protein
Helps reduce unhealthy snack cravings
Supports heart health and better metabolism
Good source of iron, magnesium, and antioxidants
Perfect for healthy snacking anytime
steps: Grind roasted buckwheat and nuts.
Add dates and blend into sticky mixture.
Mix coconut and cardamom.
Roll into small balls.
Chill and serve.

Add wet ingredients and mix well.
Pour into muffin molds.
Bake at 180°C for 20–25 minutes.
Cool and serve.

Add water to form batter.

Fry until golden brown.

Spread on tray.
Bake at 180°C for 20 minutes.
Cool and store.
